mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with federal funds rate changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where unemployment exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in real income and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration fails to predict positive equity returns specifically during recovery phases, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no negative correlation with CPIAUCSL increases over a 24-month rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls increase by more than 200 thousand in a single month, robust job creation confirms economic momentum and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surges exceeding 200 thousand fail to precede positive 60-day equity returns, or the signal generates false positives during inflationary overheating periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal income where income outpaces GDP signals expanding household purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The income-GDP divergence metric fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the relationship breaks down when consumer debt service ratios exceed historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates demand destruction and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day equity returns, or the signal loses predictive power when inventory-to-sales ratios remain below 1.0.
